summary: Independent QA review accepts the planning-only M7 Beta content and quality task graph. The DAG covers the Roadmap and acceptance-matrix M7 scope, preserves M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS and DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE, keeps required Human Gates, leaves M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001 as the only first downstream READY task while taskctl ready emits no task until graph closure, and modifies only allowed process/task/message/status paths.

tests_run.result: No output before writing this QA handoff; no unexpected dirty or untracked worktree state was present.

Diff contains PROJECT_STATUS.md, goal/model routing state, thread registry, M7 task JSON files, and M7 process messages/routes only. No apps, packages, content-source, tools, CI, roadmap, acceptance matrix, prior gate, ADR, manifest, checksum, or release artifact paths are modified.
M7-TASK-GRAPH-001 is REVIEW; M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001 is READY; the other 12 downstream M7 tasks are DRAFT.
No output while M7-TASK-GRAPH-001 remains REVIEW, so the first READY downstream task is still dependency-gated until graph closure.

tests_run.command: Get-Content -Raw -Encoding UTF8 docs/11-roadmap.md; Get-Content -Raw -Encoding UTF8 docs/23-milestone-acceptance-matrix.md; rg M7/Beta scope terms
tests_run.result: Roadmap M7 requires history/culture/language review, full scenario/person/event fill, balance and anti-snowball, performance/memory/loading, Chrome/Edge/Firefox/Windows matrix, save migration and cloud-save candidate, tutorial/hints/encyclopedia, audio/art/localization completion, and content lock. Acceptance matrix M7 requires content lock, large-scale balance/migration, platform/resolution/localization matrix, Beta culture review, and manual playthrough. The M7 DAG maps these to explicit downstream tasks.

tests_run.command: Get-Content -Raw -Encoding UTF8 docs/GATE-M6.md; rg PASS_WITH_LIMITS/DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE/manual node battle docs/28-manual-node-battle-decision.md PROJECT_STATUS.md
tests_run.result: M6 remains PASS_WITH_LIMITS, not plain PASS or release readiness. DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE remains binding for M6/M7/M8; no manual node battle implementation task or blocking spike/research/validation/exit dependency is authorized.

tests_run.command: node -e independent JSON DAG shape, acyclicity, dependency existence, status, coverage keyword, and manual-node-battle guard check
tests_run.result: DAG_CHECK_OK; M7 DAG files=14; READY=M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001; DRAFT downstream=12; graph edges=26; all dependencies exist or point to closed M6-EXIT-VALIDATION-001; no cycle found.

tests_run.command: git diff --name-only 97ec517ca96727fe7fe66e27977c3d8b0d39d9f4..HEAD -- forbidden paths; independent changed-path scope guard
tests_run.result: Forbidden-path query produced no output. Scope guard reported changed=23 and SCOPE_CHECK_OK.

M7 task files consistently carry M6 PASS_WITH_LIMITS, DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE, and Human Gate preservation notes for cultural risk, formal content lock, manual playthrough acceptance, release/commercial/branding, telemetry, server/multiplayer, cloud-save implementation/accounts/secrets, arbitrary-code mods, irreversible migration, and frozen product/platform/core changes.

decisions: Reviewed actual HEAD b3ae5ed0 because the routed workspace contains a QA route/metadata commit after 5179fda6; that extra commit is limited to allowed routing/status/task metadata and is not a blocker.
M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001 is acceptable as the only first downstream READY task, because taskctl ready emits no dependency-ready task while M7-TASK-GRAPH-001 is still REVIEW.
M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS is preserved and is not rewritten as unconditional PASS or release readiness.
DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE is preserved. The DAG creates no manual node battle implementation task and no M7/M8 blocking manual node battle spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency.

Human Gates remain explicit for major cultural-risk decisions, formal content lock acceptance, manual playthrough acceptance requiring human judgment, commercial/release/branding, telemetry, server/multiplayer, cloud-save implementation/accounts/secrets, arbitrary-code mods, irreversible migration, frozen product/platform/core changes, and any formal 1.0 scope reversal.
risks: This acceptance covers only the planning DAG. Each downstream M7 task still needs its own writer evidence, reviewer handoff, actual commands, and Human Gate handling where applicable.
The graph intentionally does not prove content quality, balance quality, platform compatibility, save migration safety, or manual playthrough success yet; those are delegated to downstream M7 tasks.
requested_action: Lead orchestrator may integrate and close M7-TASK-GRAPH-001, then route the first dependency-ready downstream task according to the accepted DAG and Human Gate constraints.
